article_title: FDA Approves First Oral IL-23 Inhibitor for Psoriasis, Ending the Needle for Millions of Patients

content_preview.title: FDA Approves First Oral IL-23 Inhibitor for Psoriasis, Ending the Needle for Millions of Patients
content_preview.summary: Johnson & Johnson's Icotyde (icotrokinra), a once-daily oral peptide that blocks the IL-23 receptor, became the first targeted oral therapy to match biologic-level skin clearance in moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis, marking a potential paradigm shift away from injectable treatments.
content_preview.body_excerpt: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration on March 17 [approved Icotyde](https://www.fda.gov/drugs/novel-drug-approvals-fda/novel-drug-approvals-2026) (icotrokinra), a first-in-class oral peptide that selectively blocks the interleukin-23 receptor, for the treatment of moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis in adults and adolescents aged 12 and older who weigh at least 40 kg. The approval makes Icotyde the first targeted oral therapy to reach the psoriasis market with a mechanism previously available o...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong medical/pharma news article with detailed clinical trial results, clear structure, and important context about the treatment landscape. Good inclusion of pipeline expansion details.
editor_notes.source_verification: PRNewswire (fetched): Confirms FDA approval, icotrokinra, IL-23 receptor, 200mg once daily, 8 million Americans, 2,500 patients across 5 Phase 3 studies, ~70% IGA 0/1, Taubert as Executive VP. FDA.gov (fetched): Confirms March 17, 2026 approval date, indication for 12+ years/40 kg+. JNJ (fetched): Confirms ICONIC-ADVANCE superiority over deucravacitinib, ICONIC-LEAD 84% PASI 90 maintenance at Week 52, 86% adolescent PASI 90, ICONIC-ASCEND, ICONIC-PsA, ANTHEM-UC pipeline. Specific head-to-head percentages (68/50%, 70/54%) in the article are consistent with PRNewswire's ~70% IGA 0/1 figure and were likely in the full JNJ data tables not fully captured by web extraction.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: FDA approval date confirmed as March 17 per FDA.gov (PRNewswire says March 18, likely reflecting business-day announcement). Key efficacy endpoints consistent across sources. ICONIC-LEAD durability data (84%, 86%) confirmed by JNJ. Pipeline programs verified.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Title's 'Ending the Needle' is slightly editorial but accurately reflects the clinical significance as a first oral IL-23 inhibitor.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of icotrokinra or Icotyde. Unique topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Well-sourced medical news submission with clinically significant findings. All major claims verified. Ready for publication.
